_note: These entries describe CARE acuity/rate factors used by DSHS to classify Adult Family Home residents under WAC 388-106-0090, -0095, -0100, -0105, -0110, and -0125. They are NOT standalone DSHS billing codes. Washington AFH Medicaid payment is set by (a) the DSHS CARE assessment producing a classification group (A-Low through E-High per WAC 388-106-0125) and (b) rate authorization by the DSHS Office of Rates Management; there is no per-factor monthly add-on payable as a discrete line item. monthly_add_on_estimate_usd is null in every entry for this reason. Specialty contracts — including Specialized Behavior Support (SBS), Residential Support Waiver (RSW), Expanded Community Services (ECS), Enhanced Adult Residential Care – Specialized Dementia Care (EARC-SDC, in ALFs), Meaningful Day, and Community Stability Supports (CSS) — are discrete DSHS-contracted programs that require AFH application, approval, training, and ongoing compliance. They are NOT automatically triggered by any single acuity factor (e.g., dementia behaviors alone do not entitle an AFH to SBS payment).

clinical_criteria: One or more behaviors enumerated in WAC 388-106-0100: assaultive behavior, combative during personal care, irritability/agitation not easily altered, hallucinations, delusions, yelling/screaming 4+ days/week, resistive behavior, repetitive verbal complaints
Behavior Point Score (BPS) above scoring thresholds in WAC 388-106-0125 (BPS >4 contributes to Group B; BPS >12 supports B-High)
Behaviors require staff intervention beyond redirection (1:1 supervision, structured behavioral care plan, documented intervention history)
factors.wac_citation: WAC 388-106-0100 (mood/behavior and behavior point score); WAC 388-106-0125 (Group B classification driver)
factors.disclosure_language_required: Per RCW 70.128.280 and DSHS Form 10-409, AFH must disclose whether it accepts residents with dementia and the specific behavioral symptoms it can safely manage (e.g., agitation, resistive care, verbal aggression). If the home cannot safely manage a behavior, the disclosure must state so. NOTE: Specialty contracts such as SBS (Specialized Behavior Support) and RSW are discrete DSHS-contracted programs requiring separate application, approval, and caregiver training; the presence of dementia behaviors does not automatically qualify an AFH for SBS/RSW reimbursement.

clinical_criteria: Cognitive Performance Score (CPS) per WAC 388-106-0090 indicating moderate to severe impairment
Documented deficits in short-term memory, daily decision-making, comprehension, or ability to make self understood
Per WAC 388-106-0125, CPS 4-6 combined with clinical complexity drives Group D; CPS below 4 with clinical complexity drives Group C
Requires structured cues, supervision during ADLs, or 24-hour orientation support
factors.wac_citation: WAC 388-106-0090 (Cognitive Performance Score); WAC 388-106-0125 (Group C/D classification driver)
factors.disclosure_language_required: Per RCW 70.128.280, AFH must disclose its capacity to provide cognitive supervision, structured routines, and a dementia-friendly environment. If the home advertises specialty dementia care, it must comply with WAC 388-76 special-care designation requirements, including caregiver training in cognitive support and disorientation management.

clinical_criteria: Depression indicator at or above the threshold used in WAC 388-106-0100 mood/behavior scoring
Crying/tearfulness 4+ days/week, anxious complaints, repetitive verbal complaints, or expressions of unrealistic fears (enumerated indicators in WAC 388-106-0100)
Coordinated mental health follow-up or psychotropic medication oversight required
factors.wac_citation: WAC 388-106-0100 (mood indicators including depression score); WAC 388-106-0125 (Group B threshold when combined with ADL or BPS)
factors.disclosure_language_required: Per RCW 70.128.280, AFH must disclose its capacity to support residents with diagnosed mood disorders, including coordination with outpatient mental health providers and PRN psychotropic medication administration. If the resident's mood symptoms exceed the home's support capability, the disclosure must state non-acceptance.

factors.acuity_factor_name: Medication administration burden (operational factor; injections are a CARE clinical-complexity criterion)
clinical_criteria: Injections — including insulin and other parenteral medications — are enumerated as clinical complexity in WAC 388-106-0095

Operational considerations not enumerated in WAC 388-106-0095: number of oral medications, multiple daily administration times, sliding scale or PRN protocols, time-sensitive regimens (Parkinson's, anti-seizure, anticoagulants). These influence staffing burden but are NOT scored CARE acuity factors.
Per WAC 388-76-10405, medications requiring administration beyond the AFH's nursing-care scope (e.g., injections by unlicensed staff) require nurse delegation under RCW 18.79.260
factors.wac_citation: WAC 388-106-0095 (clinical complexity enumerates injections; oral multi-medication administration is an operational consideration, not a scored CARE factor)
factors.disclosure_language_required: Per DSHS Form 10-409, AFH must disclose its level of medication service: (a) administration of oral/topical/eye/ear medications, (b) administration via nurse delegation under WAC 388-76-10405 and RCW 18.79.260, or (c) assistance only with resident self-administration. The home must disclose whether it manages refills, secure storage, and high-risk medications (anticoagulants, opioids, insulin).

factors.acuity_factor_name: Insulin / blood glucose monitoring acuity factor
clinical_criteria: Insulin-dependent diabetes mellitus (type 1 or insulin-requiring type 2) — Diabetes Mellitus is enumerated in the clinical complexity list in WAC 388-106-0095
Injections are independently enumerated in WAC 388-106-0095
Blood glucose monitoring 2-4 times daily; sliding-scale insulin or carbohydrate-counting regimen
History of hypoglycemic events requiring intervention
factors.wac_citation: WAC 388-106-0095 (Diabetes Mellitus — insulin and non-insulin dependent — and injections are enumerated clinical complexity factors)
factors.disclosure_language_required: Per RCW 70.128.280 and DSHS Form 10-409 (Clinical Services: 'Blood glucose monitoring'), AFH must disclose whether it performs blood glucose monitoring and whether it administers insulin, and under what authority (delegating RN, contracted nurse, or family/resident self-administration). Insulin administration by unlicensed staff requires nurse delegation per WAC 388-76-10405 and RCW 18.79.260. If insulin administration is beyond scope, the AFH must state so.

factors.acuity_factor_name: Transfer-dependence acuity factor (two-person assist / mechanical lift)
clinical_criteria: Total dependence (ADL self-performance score 4) for transfers per WAC 388-106-0105

Requires two-staff assist or mechanical lift (Hoyer, sit-to-stand) for bed-to-chair transfers (operational requirement; WAC 388-106-0105 scores transfer dependence but does not separately code two-person assist)
Cumulative ADL score >=22 combined with turning/repositioning program supports Group E (exceptional care) per WAC 388-106-0110
Documented inability to bear weight or follow transfer cues
factors.wac_citation: WAC 388-106-0105 (ADL transfer scoring); WAC 388-106-0110 (exceptional-care criteria require ADL >=22 and turning/repositioning); WAC 388-106-0125 (Group E classification)
factors.disclosure_language_required: Per DSHS Form 10-409 (Mobility/Transfers), AFH must disclose its capacity for one-on-one transfer assistance, use of lifts, belts, and transfer boards. If the home cannot safely staff a two-person transfer (e.g., single caregiver overnight), it must disclose this limitation.

clinical_criteria: Exit-seeking wandering, enumerated in WAC 388-106-0100 (behavior categories 23-28, carrying behavior values 4-5)
Non-exit-seeking wandering, enumerated in WAC 388-106-0100 (behavior categories 1-8, behavior values 0.5-1)
History of elopement or attempted elopement
Requires perimeter alarms, locked egress, or 1:1 line-of-sight supervision
factors.wac_citation: WAC 388-106-0100 (wandering enumerated; exit-seeking carries the highest behavior values); WAC 388-106-0125 (Group B classification driver)
factors.disclosure_language_required: Per RCW 70.128.280, AFH must disclose whether the physical plant has secured/alarmed egress and whether staff coverage supports a wanderer (especially overnight). If the home holds itself out as accepting dementia/wandering residents, it must comply with WAC 388-76 special-care designation requirements. SBS or RSW specialty contracts may apply to exit-seeking residents but require separate DSHS application and approval — they are not automatic.

factors.acuity_factor_name: Pressure injury / complex wound acuity factor
clinical_criteria: Pressure injuries (all stages), open lesions, stasis ulcers, and burns requiring dressing/wound care — all enumerated in WAC 388-106-0095 clinical complexity
Stage 3-4 pressure injury or unstageable wound requiring skilled-nurse-led wound protocol
Turning/repositioning program required (contributes to Group E classification per WAC 388-106-0110 when combined with ADL >=22)
factors.wac_citation: WAC 388-106-0095 (pressure injuries, open lesions, stasis ulcers, burns — clinical complexity); WAC 388-106-0110 (turning/repositioning program plus ADL >=22 supports exceptional care)
factors.disclosure_language_required: Per WAC 388-76-10405, if the resident requires nursing-level wound care, the AFH must either employ or contract with a licensed nurse to provide that care, or arrange nurse delegation. The disclosure must state the wound-care complexity the home accepts (e.g., Stage 1-2 only, or higher stages with contracted nurse).

clinical_criteria: Frequent or total bladder and/or bowel incontinence per WAC 388-106-0095
Scheduled toileting plan or bowel program in place (both enumerated in WAC 388-106-0095)
Indwelling, intermittent, or external catheter (enumerated in WAC 388-106-0095)
Ostomy care required (enumerated in WAC 388-106-0095)
factors.wac_citation: WAC 388-106-0095 (frequent/total incontinence, scheduled toileting plans, bowel programs, ostomy care, and catheter management are clinical complexity factors)
factors.disclosure_language_required: Per RCW 70.128.280, AFH must disclose its capacity to manage bowel and bladder incontinence, including frequency of brief changes, catheter care, ostomy care, and scheduled toileting. Catheter and ostomy care may require nurse delegation under WAC 388-76-10405.

clinical_criteria: Extensive assistance (ADL self-performance score 3) or total dependence (score 4) for toilet use per WAC 388-106-0105
Requires staff assist for transfer-to-toilet, brief changes, peri-care
Documented falls or near-falls during toileting attempts
factors.disclosure_language_required: Per DSHS Form 10-409 (Personal Hygiene: 'assistance provided, one on one guide, pericare'), AFH must disclose its level of toileting assistance (independent prompting, hands-on assist, or total dependence). The disclosure must align with the negotiated care plan.

clinical_criteria: Extensive assistance or total dependence for eating (ADL self-performance score 3-4) per WAC 388-106-0105
Swallowing problems with eating dependence — enumerated in WAC 388-106-0095 clinical complexity
Modified-texture diet (puree, mechanical soft) or thickened liquids (operational consideration)
Tube feeding providing >50% of calories supports Group E exceptional-care classification per WAC 388-106-0110
factors.wac_citation: WAC 388-106-0105 (ADL scoring — eating); WAC 388-106-0095 (swallowing problems with eating dependence — clinical complexity); WAC 388-106-0110 (tube feeding >50% of calories supports exceptional care)
factors.disclosure_language_required: Per DSHS Form 10-409 (Nutrition/Feeding: 'Spoon feeding, blended diets, sugar free, fiber accommodations'), AFH must disclose feeding levels it supports: independent, set-up only, hands-on assist, spoon-feeding, and texture-modified diets. Tube feeding capability must be explicitly disclosed and may require nurse delegation under WAC 388-76-10405.

factors.acuity_factor_name: Fall risk acuity factor (composite — not separately enumerated in CARE)
clinical_criteria: Pathological bone fracture is enumerated in WAC 388-106-0095 clinical complexity
ADL locomotion score (locomotion in room, locomotion outside room, walk in room) per WAC 388-106-0105 — the highest of the three contributes to ADL totals

Operational clinical context (not separately enumerated in CARE): two or more falls in the prior 30 days, fall with injury, gait instability requiring an assistive device, cognitive-mobility compound risk
Fall risk per se is not an independent CARE acuity factor — it manifests through enumerated factors (locomotion ADL, pathological bone fracture, and CPS where cognition contributes)
factors.wac_citation: WAC 388-106-0095 (pathological bone fracture — clinical complexity); WAC 388-106-0105 (ADL locomotion scoring)
factors.disclosure_language_required: Per RCW 70.128.280 and DSHS Form 10-409 (Mobility/Transfers), AFH must disclose its fall-prevention measures (bed alarms, low beds, perimeter monitoring), supervised ambulation availability, and whether 1:1 staffing during high-risk transfers is feasible given staffing ratios.
